research.summary: HIGH SUSPENSION RISK VERTICAL. Google's 2026 spam crackdown targets locksmiths specifically — sector has the highest GBP suspension rates due to historical 'profile farms' (fake/manipulated listings). Top suspension trigger in 2026: keyword stuffing in business name (e.g., 'Culver City Emergency Locksmith 24/7' instead of legal name). Legitimate locksmiths must operate with extra rigor on naming, NAP consistency, and review authenticity — Google's automated detection flags violations at scale and reinstatement can take weeks.
research.recommendedPrimaryCategoryReasoning: Empirically dominant in Culver City top-5 (all use 'Locksmith'). Canonical GBP category. NO keyword variants — keep business name as the legal name, never modify for SEO.

topRankingFactors.benchmark: Business name must match legal/displayed name exactly. NO 'Emergency', 'Best', '24/7' or city stuffing.
Identical name+address+phone on every listing. Mismatch is a top suspension trigger.
Solicit ethically via SMS post-job. Incentivized reviews trigger suspension.
Multiple listings at same address = automatic suspension. Audit + clean before optimization.
Don't claim service in cities you can't reach within 1 hour. Abusive service areas trigger flags.
LocalBusiness schema + clear license number on website + GBP. Builds legitimacy signal.

commonPitfalls: Keyword-stuffed business name ('Emergency Locksmith 24/7 [City]') — #1 suspension cause in 2026
Duplicate listings at same address (often unintentional after rebrand)
Abusive service areas (claiming cities you don't actually serve)
Missing license number on website + GBP
Generic placeholder photos vs real shop/team photos (flagged as 'fake' signal)
research.titleTagExample: Locksmith in Culver City, CA | Acme Lock & Key
research.h1Example: Licensed Locksmith in Culver City — 24/7 Lockout Service
research.metaDescExample: Licensed locksmith in Culver City, CA. 30-min response, residential, commercial, automotive lockouts. License #LSL12345. Call (310) 555-0200.
voiceoverHooks: No service-area pages for multi-city operators
voiceoverAntipatterns: DO NOT suggest adding 'Emergency' or location keywords to business name — that's the #1 suspension trigger in this vertical.
DO NOT advise broader service areas — abusive boundaries flag suspension.
DO NOT cite incentivized review tactics.
clientOnboardingChecklist: Audit business name for keyword stuffing (must be legal name) — FIX BEFORE ANY OPTIMIZATION
Verify single GBP listing per physical location (delete duplicates)
Add appropriate secondary categories (Auto locksmith, Safe supplier, Key duplication) only if applicable
Fill all 12 service attributes that match real services
Display license number on GBP description + website footer
Set realistic service area (1-hour drive radius max)
Upload 20+ AUTHENTIC photos (real shop, team, vans, completed work)
Set complete hours including 24/7 emergency if offered
Set up ETHICAL automated SMS review request (no incentives)
Build citation profile across 13 targets — verify NAP exact match (critical)
Add LocalBusiness schema with full NAP + service area + license
Establish review response process — target ≥90% response rate
Build per-city service area landing pages where they actually serve

kpiTargets.rankingTimeline: Map Pack 60-90 days; longer due to extra suspension-prevention audits

industryNotes: Google's 2026 crackdown disproportionately impacts locksmiths — collateral suspensions of legitimate businesses are real.
Reinstatement after suspension: days to weeks. Better to prevent.
Profile farms historically dominated 'near me' locksmith searches in major cities — Google now sweeping aggressively.
License display is a legitimacy signal Google's manual reviewers look for.
consumesIn: FUTURE: Brain client-strategy module — locksmith clients need suspension-prevention audit FIRST
FUTURE: admin onboarding wizard with name-compliance check
FUTURE: portal KPI dashboard with kpiTargets thresholds
